task_type.description: Profile selector for profile-aware plugins (e.g. development v0.2.0). Set by clarifier output frontmatter; null/missing means the plugin's default profile is used.

profile.description: Resolved profile name (cached by orchestrator-tick after the first dispatch). Internal.

parent_id.description: M10 task-chain link: user-confirmed parent task in the same workspace; null = independent.

chain_root.description: M10 cached terminal ancestor of the chain (O(1) root lookup); MUST equal walk_ancestors(...).last when parent_id != null, else null.

model_override.description: v0.18 — per-task LLM model override. Highest-priority layer in the C/B/A/D resolution chain (task > phase > plugin > workspace). Opaque string forwarded verbatim to the conductor.

execution_mode.description: v0.19 — per-task execution dispatch mode. 'sub-agent' (default) emits phase_prompt envelopes; 'inline' emits inline_dispatch envelopes that the conductor handles in-process. Absence ⇒ default 'sub-agent'.

task_input.description: v0.20 — initial task seed text supplied via `task new --input` / `--input-file` / `--interactive`. Surfaced verbatim in the tick dispatch envelope under the same key so phase agents and the inline conductor can use it.

entry_answers.description: v0.29.3 — plugin-defined entry-question answers. Keys correspond to fields declared in `plugins/<id>/entry-questions.yaml :: <phase>.required`. Populated via `codenook task set --field <key> --value <answer>` when the field is not in the kernel's reserved writable allow-list. Read by `check_entry_questions()` (with top-level fallback for back-compat).
